messages.subject: [R-gui] Good Tcl/Tk widget reference?
messages.body_plain:
On 9/16/10 4:22 PM, "Hodgess, Erin" <HodgessE at uhd.edu> wrote:

> Next, what is a good reference for Tcl/tk that would include the options
> for widgets, please?

<http://www.amazon.com/Effective-Tcl-Tk-Programming-Programs/dp/0201634740>
"Effective Tcl/Tk Programming" (Harrison/McLennan)
    Best Tk book available (GUI programming).

<http://www.beedub.com/book/>
"Practical Programming in Tcl and Tk" (Welch/Jones)
    Everything. Good reference in general.


Both are must-haves for doing any significant amount of Tcl/Tk work.
I found John Ousterhout's book to be more dense and of less
value than either listed above.


<Sorry so late to the party...>

messages.subject: [R-gui] Tcl/Tk Binding % Substitutions.for Callbacks
messages.body_plain:
<previously misfiled in r-devel...>

How are % substitutions handled for callbacks in R?
Is there an 'event' parameter that can be requested
which would contain the rest of the information specific
to the event type itself?

How should we translate something like the following:

Tcl> bind Frame <Enter> {%W config -bg red}
Tcl> bind Frame <Leave> {%W config -bg white}

such that the widget id (and other % substitutions) can be accessed
in R callback? I'm trying to write callback handlers for dynamic
bindtags.

one   <- tkframe(width=30, height=30)
two   <- tkframe(width=30, height=30)
three <- tkframe(width=30, height=30)
tkbind("Frame",
       "<Enter>",
       function(TBD} {
           ## How do I determine widget id (%W) here?
       })

messages.subject: [R-gui] Tcl/Tk Binding % Substitutions.for Callbacks
messages.body_plain:
Paul:

On 02/23/2011 02:49 PM, Roebuck,Paul L wrote:
> <previously misfiled in r-devel...>
> 
> How are % substitutions handled for callbacks in R?
> Is there an 'event' parameter that can be requested
> which would contain the rest of the information specific
> to the event type itself?
> 
> How should we translate something like the following:
> 
> Tcl> bind Frame <Enter> {%W config -bg red}
> Tcl> bind Frame <Leave> {%W config -bg white}
> 
> such that the widget id (and other % substitutions) can be accessed
> in R callback? I'm trying to write callback handlers for dynamic
> bindtags.
> 
> one   <- tkframe(width=30, height=30)
> two   <- tkframe(width=30, height=30)
> three <- tkframe(width=30, height=30)
> tkbind("Frame",
>        "<Enter>",
>        function(TBD} {
>            ## How do I determine widget id (%W) here?
>        })

I think you can just use the letter without % as a function argument, try

library(tcltk)
tt <- tktoplevel()
tkbind(tt,'<Enter>',function(W){
  cat(paste('Toplevel',W,'\n'))
})

Greetings,

Adrian


>

> R-SIG-GUI mailing list
> R-SIG-GUI at r-project.org
> https://stat.ethz.ch/mailman/listinfo/r-sig-gui
